machinery_genes.quote: the xpsD gene, which encodes an outer membrane protein that serves as the secretion channel by forming multimers
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2000, X. campestris pv. campestris xps T2SS. The same gene is named and mutated in S. maltophilia K279a: Karaba et al 2013 constructed 'an xpsD (Smlt0697) mutant (NUS2)' (PMID 23774603, DOI 10.1128/IAI.00546-13). GspD-family secretin.

machinery_genes.quote: ATP binding triggers oligomerization of Xanthomonas campestris XpsE (a GspE homolog) as well as its association with the N-terminal domain of XpsL (a GspL homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Shiue et al 2006, EMBO J. XpsE is the GspE-family secretion ATPase of the X. campestris xps T2SS; its ATPase function further characterized in Shiue et al 2007 (PMID 17630971, DOI 10.1111/j.1365-2958.2007.05795.x).

machinery_genes.quote: a homology exists between TcpE and the Klebsiella pneumoniae pullulanase secretion proteins PulD and PulF, the Xanthomonas campestris extracellular enzyme secretion factor XpsF
machinery_genes.note: XpsF named as a PulF(GspF)-family component of the X. campestris extracellular secretion machinery. The orthologue is named and mutated in S. maltophilia K279a: Karaba et al 2013 built 'A deletion mutant (NUS4) of xpsF (Smlt0688)' and showed Xps-dependent secretion (PMID 23774603, DOI 10.1128/IAI.00546-13). This xpsF mutant is the K279a strain-transfer anchor for the whole Xps system.

machinery_genes.quote: the XpsG protein of Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris ... is a pillar-like structure spanning the cytoplasmic and outer membranes
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 2002 (Biochem J). Title designates XpsG 'the major pseudopilin'; GspG family.

machinery_genes.quote: a linearly ordered interactive relationship is revealed among the four pseudopilins, XpsG-XpsI-XpsH-XpsJ
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005, X. campestris pv. campestris; XpsH named as a minor pseudopilin of the xps T2SS (GspH family).

machinery_genes.quote: a linearly ordered interactive relationship is revealed among the four pseudopilins, XpsG-XpsI-XpsH-XpsJ
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; XpsI named as a minor pseudopilin of the xps T2SS (GspI family).

machinery_genes.quote: characterize the specialized minor pseudopilin, XpsJ ... XpsJ might act as a tip to restrict the out-growth of XpsG
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; XpsJ named as the specialized minor (tip) pseudopilin of the xps T2SS (GspJ family).

machinery_genes.quote: we observed mutual dependence for normal steady-state levels between the XpsL and the XpsM proteins
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2001, X. campestris pv. campestris. XpsL is the GspL-family IM-platform protein; binds the XpsE ATPase via its N-terminal cytoplasmic domain (Shiue et al 2006, PMID 16525507).

machinery_genes.quote: we observed mutual dependence for normal steady-state levels between the XpsL and the XpsM proteins
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2001. XpsM is the GspM-family IM-platform protein; forms the reversibly dissociable XpsL-XpsM-XpsN ternary complex (Tsai et al 2002, PMID 12123417, DOI 10.1042/BJ20020909).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (GspC; links IM platform to secretin XpsD)
machinery_genes.quote: XpsL, XpsM, and XpsN are components of such machinery in the plant pathogen Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2004, titled 'Functional dissection of the XpsN (GspC) protein'. XpsN is the GspC-family bitopic IM protein that associates with the secretin XpsD (Lee et al 2000, PMID 10692359) and with the major pseudopilin XpsG (Lee et al 2004, PMID 15590656, DOI 10.1074/jbc.M409362200).

machinery_genes.role: type IV prepilin (leader) peptidase (matures pseudopilins; shared with type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: Sequencing of the cloned fragment revealed an open reading frame (ORF) (designated xpsO) of 287 amino acid residues. The amino acid sequence shares 50% identity with that of PilD
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 1995, X. campestris pv. campestris. XpsO = the type IV prepilin leader peptidase (PilD/XcpA / GspO family), required for extracellular protein secretion; functionally complements the P. aeruginosa pilD mutant. Encoded outside the xpsE-N operon but ties to the Xps T2SS apparatus.

notes: System: the Xps type II secretion system of Stenotrophomonas maltophilia K279a (sys_instance_label 'Xps (xpsE-N)'), the system that secretes the serine-protease effectors StmPr1/StmPr2/StmPr3 (e.g. Smlt0861). The Xps system in K279a was established as a functional T2SS by Karaba, White & Cianciotto 2013 (PMID 23774603), who named and mutated xpsD (Smlt0697) and xpsF (Smlt0688) in this exact strain; that paper provides the K279a strain anchor. Gene-by-gene apparatus characterization, however, was almost entirely done in the closely related Xanthomonadaceae model Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris (Hu/Leu/Chen lab), whose xps cluster shares the same xps prefix and gene order; component-naming quotes are therefore taken from those X. campestris papers and transferred to the orthologous K279a Xps system (strain transfer noted per gene). Core apparatus fully covered by quotes: secretin (XpsD/GspD), secretion ATPase (XpsE/GspE), inner-membrane platform (XpsC-less; XpsF/GspF, XpsL/GspL, XpsM/GspM, XpsN/GspC), major + minor pseudopilins (XpsG, XpsH, XpsI, XpsJ), and the type IV prepilin peptidase (XpsO/GspO). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: the secreted serine-protease substrates StmPr1/StmPr2/StmPr3 (effectors, not machinery); the second, cryptic Gsp T2SS of K279a (Karaba 2013 found it non-functional under all conditions tested) so its gsp genes are not part of the active Xps apparatus; the Sec/Tat translocon and any transcriptional regulators. LOW-CONFIDENCE / OMITTED on purpose: XpsK (minor pseudopilin) - the GspK-family member is referenced only by the unified pseudopilin family list 'GspG, -H, -I, -J and -K' (Hu et al 2002 abstract) and I found no quote naming an Xps-prefixed 'XpsK' specifically, so it is left out rather than padded. XpsC - the K279a locus is described as running 'xpsC through xpsM' (Karaba 2013) but the Xanthomonas operon in the corpus label is xpsE-N and no individual XpsC naming/characterization quote was found; omitted. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts/PMC entries or (for the K279a xpsD/xpsF locus-tag quotes) the Karaba 2013 PMC full text; every PMID was confirmed real via get_article_metadata and every DOI is resolvable.
